flavour:
The Palladium and Sapphire class battlecruisers are more specialised than their conventionally aggressive kin and were conceived for disruption and close-range engagements. Although both classes aren't commonly encountered, the Palladium is rarer than the Sapphire. Why this should be is uncertain, although the generally accepted theory is that the Adamant/ Palladium type hull is an ancient design no longer manufactured, having been superseded by the more recent Ruby/ Sapphire type.
These vessels rely on a pair of ion storm generator banks to inflict crippling damage – equally devastating in the close action or orbital bombardment roles. Their second main armament is a pair of gravity coils. These forward-facing constructs create a localised, deadly beam of gravitic disturbance, ripping chunks from hulls, crushing bulkheads under their own weight, and even changing the heading of a multi-thousand-ton warship, disrupting the battle plans of any foe.
